reviews.review_msg: Professor Crowe is outstanding. Very passionate toward the subjects he teaches. Would deffinitely take a course with him. He tries to make lectures engaging and interesting.
a very interesting class. he really wants all of the students to understand, but don't worry, the stuff that is really confusing is not testable.
if u attended to his class, you should be fine!
He's in love with Confucian philosophy. Only take a course by him if it's SUPPOSED to be about eastern philosophy, because no matter what he teaches, it always comes back to Confucius and Mencius.

Too focused on historical aspects, glosses over a lot of the actual 'cultural' parts, such as the effect of XYZ on Chinese culture.  Classes started off mostly relevant, starting about the third week, he's been so carried away by philosophy/religion he misses every other topic on his outline.
cares about the subject matter and trying to get students interested.

The class is "intro to chinese culture" but he's morphed it into "intro to confucian philosophy." He uses LOTS of circular arguments and repeats himself over and over and over and over. Bring a pillow to class, cause desks are hard.
His lecture notes are so messy and require you to write your own notes. he does not post any notes online.

Going to lecture is a must as he doesn't record himself & his notes are minimal... Quizzes are not too hard if you study; however, he likes to ask really obscure questions, and his questions are not very specific. The course topics are all over the place and extremely uninteresting towards the end.

Super nice guy but WEIRD. Topics all over the map. "Term paper" written IN CLASS. Wastes whole lecture on essay basics (This isn't ENG 100!). Docked points for not starting thesis statement with "In this essay" (seriously!) Tests focus on a lot of pointless obscurities. Vague questions, but docks points if you don't list details he's looking for

He's a great teacher and he teaches well. His quizzes are difficult and he marks on the hard side though. Going to class is a good idea since he doesn't do recorded lectures or online notes. If you take his classes, please remember to keep up with the lectures and readings.
